title: Resend A Geek+ Command From The API Screen
summary: Use the Resend Geek Command control on the System HMI API screen AGVs section during documented system recovery handling when Geek+ has a command error and the command guide can be provided so the message is resent to Geek+.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during system recovery handling when Geek+ has a command error and the command guide can be provided, using the Resend Geek Command control in the AGVs section of the API screen.
not_for: Do not use when the command guide is unavailable.
Do not use when the source does not support the exact command guide contents or entry method for the situation being handled.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the recovery-related resend action from the System HMI API screen AGVs section when a Geek+ command error is being handled and the command guide can be provided.

safety_notes: This is a recovery-related action and is not marked support-safe by the candidate.
The source does not define the command guide format, contents, or entry method; use caution and escalate if those details are not known.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the System HMI API screen
AGVs section of the API screen

access_required: Access to the System HMI API screen
system_preconditions: A Geek+ command error is being handled.
The AGVs section of the API screen is available.
The command guide can be provided.

visual_references.description: AGV API Controls screen showing the AGVs section of the API screen and the Resend Geek Command control.
ACB API screen reference showing navigation context for the API interface where AGV controls are accessed.

steps.title: Open the AGVs section of the API screen
steps.purpose: Access the AGV API controls area where the Resend Geek Command function is available.
steps.instruction: Open the System HMI API screen and go to the AGVs section.
steps.expected_result: The AGVs section of the API screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGVs section is visible and the AGV API controls are available on screen.
steps.failure_condition: The API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed or is not visible.

actions.label: Open AGVs API section
actions.exact_action: Open the AGVs section of the API screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: API interface context used to access system, AGV, and tote controls.
AGV API Controls screen showing the AGVs section where Resend Geek Command is located.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the System HMI API screen cannot be accessed.
Stop or escalate if the AGVs section is not available.

source_evidence.text: Open the AGVs section of the API screen.
Figure 4-12 is associated with the AGVs section of the API screen and documents controls including Resend Geek Command.
Users press API on the ACB System screen to access the ACB API screen, from which AGVs can be controlled.

steps.title: Locate the Resend Geek Command control
steps.purpose: Identify the specific recovery-related control used to resend the message to Geek+.
steps.instruction: In the AGVs section of the API screen, locate the Resend Geek Command control.
steps.expected_result: The Resend Geek Command control is identified on the AGV API Controls screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Resend Geek Command control is visible and selectable.
steps.failure_condition: The Resend Geek Command control cannot be found on the AGV API Controls screen.

actions.label: Find resend control
actions.exact_action: Locate the Resend Geek Command control.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Resend Geek Command control within the AGV API Controls screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Resend Geek Command control is not present.
Stop or escalate if the displayed screen does not match the AGV API Controls view.

source_evidence.text: Locate the Resend Geek Command control.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resend Geek Command: This is also used for a system recover.

steps.title: Provide the command guide
steps.purpose: Supply the command guide required by the documented recovery behavior when a Geek+ command error exists.
steps.instruction: Provide the command guide as described by the source when handling a Geek+ command error.
steps.expected_result: The command guide is provided to the Resend Geek Command function.
steps.healthy_condition: The command guide is available and can be provided as part of the resend action.
steps.failure_condition: The command guide is unavailable, incomplete, or the source-supported method for providing it is not known.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The AGV API Controls screen where the Resend Geek Command function is documented; the source does not define the command guide entry field or method.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the command guide is unavailable.
Stop or escalate if the source-supported command guide format or entry method is not known.

source_evidence.text: Provide the command guide as described by the source when a Geek+ command error is being handled.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If Geek+ has a command error, the command guide can be provided and the message will be resent to Geek+.

steps.title: Resend the message to Geek+
steps.purpose: Execute the documented recovery action so the message is resent to Geek+.
steps.instruction: Use the Resend Geek Command control so the message is resent to Geek+.
steps.expected_result: The message is resent to Geek+ using the documented recovery-related control.
steps.healthy_condition: The resend action completes and the message is resent to Geek+.
steps.failure_condition: The message cannot be resent successfully or the resend action does not complete.

actions.label: Resend Geek command
actions.exact_action: Use the Resend Geek Command control so the message is resent to Geek+.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Resend Geek Command control used to resend the message to Geek+.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the message cannot be resent successfully.
Stop or escalate if the control does not produce the documented resend behavior.

source_evidence.text: Use the Resend Geek Command control so the message is resent to Geek+.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resend Geek Command: This is also used for a system recover. If Geek+ has a command error, the command guide can be provided and the message will be resent to Geek+.

success_criteria: The AGVs section of the API screen is accessed.
The Resend Geek Command control is located.
The message is resent to Geek+.
healthy_conditions: The AGV API Controls screen is available.
The Resend Geek Command control is visible.
The command guide is available for the recovery action.
The resend action results in the message being resent to Geek+.
failure_conditions: The System HMI API screen or AGVs section cannot be accessed.
The Resend Geek Command control cannot be located.
The source does not define the command guide format or entry method.
The message cannot be resent successfully.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the command guide is unavailable.
Escalate if the message cannot be resent successfully.
Escalate for SME review if the required command guide contents, format, or entry method are not known from the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AGV API Controls screen showing the Resend Geek Command control in the AGVs section.
API screen context used to access AGV controls.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Resend Geek Command: This is also used for a system recover. If Geek+ has a command error, the command guide can be provided and the message will be resent to Geek+.

missing_details: The source does not define the command guide contents.
The source does not define how the command guide is entered or provided in the HMI.
The source does not define confirmation indicators for a successful resend.
The source does not define follow-up verification steps after the message is resent.
The source does not state whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not state whether lockout/tagout is required.
